articles.title: Charges ignored for former Flyers star accused of attacking girlfriend
articles.description: Jun. 5—A Montgomery County grand jury has decided to ignore charges for a former University of Dayton basketball star accused of attacking a woman late Sunday, May 3 in Dayton. Roosevelt Chapman Jr., 64, was previously charged in Dayton Municipal Court with abduction and misdemeanor assault. According to an affidavit filed in the case, Chapman approached a police officer at about 11:22 p.m. ...

articles.title: Dayton basketball: Flyers will play Patriot League opponent in December
articles.description: Jun. 5—The Dayton Flyers men's basketball team will play Loyola University Maryland on Dec. 2 at UD Arena, according to a report on Friday, June 5, by college basketball analyst Rocco Miller. The Flyers lead the series against Loyola 3-0. The programs last played on Dec. 22, 2007. Dayton won 91-74 at UD Arena. The first two games in the series took place in 1950 and 1951. Loyola finished 12-20 ...

articles.description: Erick All Jr. worried his career might be over back in 2024 when he re-tore the ACL he injured the year prior in his final college season at Iowa. articles.title: 'Leader' Happe signs two-year deal with Aberdeen
articles.description: Aberdeen manager Stephen Robinson believes new signing Dan Happe will bring leadership and aerial dominance to the Dons' defence. The 27-year-old centre-back has signed a two-year deal at Pittodrie - with the option for a third - and will join Aberdeen when his contract at Leyton Orient expires later this summer. Happe has spent his entire senior career with Orient, making over 250 appearances and earning promotion twice.
